title: Rustic Kitchen Island
summary: A kitchen island on four built-up 2x4 legs — an inner post wrapped by three stacked outer pieces — with a slatted 2x4 bottom shelf and a planked 2x8 tabletop.
description:
Each leg is built up from four pieces rather than cut from a single post: one full-height inner 2x4, wrapped by a bottom, middle, and top outer 2x4 piece stacked to fill out the leg's visible face. **Cut the three outer pieces to fit your own assembled frame** — treat the given lengths as a starting point, since small variances in the aprons and shelf supports change exactly where each outer piece needs to land.

Side and end aprons join the four legs into a single base frame, with shelf supports running between them to carry the twelve-board slatted bottom shelf — leave consistent gaps between the shelf boards for the plank look and for airflow underneath.

The tabletop is four 2x8 boards run the long way and glued/screwed edge to edge — **this rustic plank top is meant to show its board lines**, so keep the boards tight but don't fill or hide the seams the way a glued-up furniture top would.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the base frame
steps.body: Join the four inner legs with the side and end aprons and the shelf supports.

steps.title: Build up the legs
steps.body: Cut the bottom, middle, and top outer leg pieces to fit your own assembled frame and attach around each inner leg.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the twelve shelf boards across the shelf supports with consistent gaps between them.

steps.title: Build the tabletop
steps.body: Glue and screw the four 2x8 tabletop boards edge to edge, then attach to the base.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Rustic kitchen island with built-up legs and plank top
